preview_text:
The model assigns Croatia a dominant 81% win probability, leaving Ghana with just a 2% chance of victory. This represents a decisive forecast heavily favoring the European side, with the underdog positioned as a substantial outsider.

The spread across outcomes reveals a match the model expects to be largely one-sided. Croatia's win probability towers over both the draw at 17% and Ghana's slim upset window, suggesting the model anticipates a fairly comfortable result rather than a closely contested encounter.

The model and sportsbook diverge meaningfully here. Books price Croatia's win at 54%, some 27 percentage points below the model's 81%. Conversely, books give Ghana 20% implied probability versus the model's 2%—an 18-point gap in the opposite direction. The draw sits closer together (17% model, 26% books), but the underdog's probability spreads remain substantial. The dashboard presents both views without claiming certainty about which better reflects true match dynamics.
